hero.label: Open source · Self-hosted · Zero backend
hero.title: See every attack your server blocks
hero.subtitle: Real-time fail2ban intelligence. No database. No build step. Just static files.

highlights.label: Why f2b-dashboard
highlights.title: Built for servers, not for clouds
highlights.subtitle: A dashboard that respects your infrastructure. No containers to tune, no databases to back up.
highlights.selfHostedDesc: All data stays on your server. No external telemetry, no third-party dashboards.
highlights.noDatabaseDesc: Results are written as static JSON. Cron refreshes them on your schedule.
highlights.staticWebDesc: Pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Open it with any static file server or directly in a browser.
highlights.geoIpDesc: Attack sources are mapped to countries and cities using the free IP-API tier.

features.label: Features
features.title: Maps, timelines, and jail reports
features.subtitle: Everything you want to know about incoming attacks, organized in one place.
features.timelineDesc: See attack volume over time with interactive ECharts visualizations and time-range filtering.
features.mapDesc: Geo-locate attackers on a world map. Spot patterns by country and region at a glance.
features.jailDesc: Dive into each fail2ban jail: top IPs, ban ratio, unique attackers, and configuration.
features.logsDesc: Browse and filter recent fail2ban events without leaving the dashboard.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.title: Four moving parts. Zero magic.
howItWorks.subtitle: The parser reads your logs, GeoIP enriches the IPs, and the static UI renders the JSON.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Your existing fail2ban log file is the only data source.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Aggregates bans, unbans, found events, and jail stats into JSON.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Looks up each IP with IP-API and caches results locally.
howItWorks.step4Desc: The static dashboard renders the enriched JSON with ECharts.

quickStart.label: Quick start
quickStart.title: Run it in two minutes
quickStart.subtitle: Clone the repo to /opt, run the parser once, and open the dashboard. Then add a cron job to keep it fresh.
quickStart.step1Desc: Install into /opt/f2b-dashboard and make the scripts executable.
quickStart.step2Desc: Generate dashboard.json from your logs, then add GeoIP data.
quickStart.step3Desc: Let the scripts refresh automatically every 5 minutes.
quickStart.note1: Note: installing under /opt/f2b-dashboard requires root privileges.
quickStart.note2: Run as root, or prefix each command with sudo.
quickStart.section4: Serve web/ with any static server
